Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, [ch 5, sc] in ea ch-2 sp around, ch 2, dc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”24 ch-sps.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, sc around post of dc, *[ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp] twice, ch 5, [4 dc-cl, ch 5, 4 dc-cl] in next ch-5 sp, ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”30 ch-sps.

stitches.description: This timeless hexagon is elegant and intricate. When combined together in a tile pattern, this motif makes a stunning fabric.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 12 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”12 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc in 1st sc, *ch 7, sk 1 ch, sc in next sc; rep from * around to last sc, ch 2, sk last sc, dtr in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 3 (counts as dc), 4 dc around post of dtr, [ch 3, 5 dc] in ea ch-7 sp around, ch 3, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”30 dc.
Rnd 4: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next 4 dc, *ch 3, dc in next 5 dc; rep from * around, ch 3, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”30 dc.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, dc4tog over next 4 dc, *[ch 3, sc in next ch-3 sp] twice, ch 3, dc5tog over next 5 dc; rep from * around, [ch 3, sc in next ch-3 sp] 8 times, ch 3, sl st to top of dc4tog, do not turnâ€”18 ch-sps.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, [ch 5, sc] in ea ch-sp around, ch 5, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”18 ch-sps.
Rnd 7: Sl st to ch-5 sp, [4 dc, ch 3, 4 dc] in same ch-5 sp, ch 3, *[4 dc, ch 3, 4 dc] in next ch-5 sp, ch 3; rep from * around to last 2 ch-5 sps, [4 dc, ch 3, 4 dc] in next ch-5 sp, ch 3, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten offâ€”60 dc.

stitches.description: This hexagon is like a stained glass design inside a beautiful window.
stitches.instructions:
Make an adjustable ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 2, 2 dc-cl in ring, [ch 4, 3 dc-cl] 3 times in ring, ch 1, dc in top of 2 dc-cl, pull ring closed, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, 4 sc around post of dc, 6 sc in ea ch-4 sp, 2 sc in 1st ch-sp, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”24 sc.
Rnd 3: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next 2 sc, [ch 5, sk 1 sc, dc in next 3 sc] 5 times, ch 2, dc in top of t-ch (counts as ch-sp), do not turnâ€”18 dc.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, 5 sc around post of dc, sk t-ch, *sc in next dc, 9 sc in next ch-5 sp (arch made), sk 1 dc; rep from * around, sc in next dc, 4 sc in 1st ch-sp, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 arches.
Rnd 5: Ch 6 (counts as dc, ch-3 sp), dc in 1st sc, *ch 4, dtr2tog over prev sc and middle sc of next arch, ch 4, [dc, ch 3, dc] in same sc; rep from * around, ch 4, dtr2tog over prev sc and middle sc of next arch, ch 4, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”18 ch-sps.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, *5 sc in next ch-sp, 4 sc in next ch-4 sp, sc around dtr2tog, 4 sc in next ch-4 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”84 sc.
Rnd 7: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next sc, *3 dc in next sc, dc in next 13 sc; rep from * around, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”96 dc.

stitches.description: The lace of this motif is spotted with clusters that can look like lattice with blossoms when combined with more crystal lace motifs.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 2, 2 dc-cl in ring, [ch 3, 3 dc-cl] 7 times in ring, ch 1, hdc in top of 2 dc-cl, do not turnâ€”8 dc-cl.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, [ch 5, sc] in ea ch-3 sp around, ch 2, dc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 sc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc around post of dc, *ch 5, [3 dc-cl, ch 3, 3 dc-cl] in next ch-5 sp, ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around to last ch-5 sp, ch 5, [3 dc-cl, ch 3, 3 dc-cl] in next ch-5 sp, ch 2, dc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 dc-cl.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc around post of dc, *ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp, ch 5, [sc, ch 5, sc] in next ch-3 sp, ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 ch-5 sps.

stitches.description: The circle of clusters is highlighted on the last round with chain spaces.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 5 (counts as dc, ch-2 sp), [dc, ch 2] 7 times in ring, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”8 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 3, 3 dc-cl in ch-2 sp, [ch 4, 4 dc-cl] in ea ch-2 sp, ch 2, hdc in top of 3 dc-cl, do not turnâ€”8 dc-cl.
Rnd 3: Ch 3 (counts as dc), 2 dc around post of hdc, *[ch 3, (sc, ch 5) twice, sc, ch 3] in next ch-4 sp, [3 dc, ch 5, 3 dc] in next ch-4 sp; rep from * to last ch-4 sp, [ch 3, (sc, ch 5) twice, sc, ch 3] in next ch-4 sp, 3 dc in last ch-sp, ch 5, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten offâ€”20 ch-sps.

stitches.description: This bulky cross is encircled with a delicate line of picot chains. It's a beautiful motif that makes a delicate fabric when combined together.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 16 sc in ring, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, *sc in next 2 sc, [sc, ch 9, sc] in next sc, sc in next sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”20 sc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, *sc in sc, [2 hdc, 17 dc, 2 hdc] in ch-9 sp, sk 2 sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”68 dc.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, *sc spike in sc on rnd 2, ch 5, sk 5 sts, [sc in next dc, picot, ch 5, sk 4 dc] twice, sc in next dc, picot, ch 5; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 ch-sps.

stitches.description: In this quirky motif, the single crochet around the last round shows off the zigzag of the chain spaces perfectly.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 8, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), *dc in ring, picot, 2 dc in ring; rep from * 6 more times, [dc , picot, dc] in ring, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”24 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc in top of t-ch, *ch 6, sk 2 dc, sc in next dc; rep from * around, ch 6, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-6 sps.
Rnd 3: *[3 sc, ch 3, sc, ch 3, 3 sc] in next ch-6 sp, ch 9, [3 sc, ch 3, sc, ch 3, 3 sc] in next ch-6 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”4 ch-9 sps.

stitches.description: The colorful flower trapped inside this motif can be sculptural with a thicker yarn and small hook, or lie flat with heavy blocking.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 5, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 8 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, [sc, ch 4] in ea sc around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 sc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, [sc, hdc, 3 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-4 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off color, do not turnâ€”8 petals.
Rnd 4: Join new color to sl st, ch 1, *sc between next 2 sc on prev rnd, ch 6; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-sps.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, [sc, hdc, 5 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-6 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off color, do not turnâ€”8 petals.
Rnd 6: Join new color with sl st btw 1st 2 dc on next petal, sc in same sp, *ch 5, sc btw 4th and 5th dc on petal, ch 5, sc btw 1st and 2nd dc on next petal; rep from * around to last petal, ch 5, sc btw 4th and 5th dc on last petal, ch 2, dc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 ch-sps.
Rnd 7: Ch 1, sc around post of dc, *ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp, [ch 2, 4 dc, ch 3, 4 dc, ch 2] in next ch-5 sp, sc in next ch-5 sp, ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”20 ch-sps.

stitches.description: This beautiful motif makes a great fabric when turned on its side into diamonds.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 4 (counts as dc, ch-1 sp), [dc, ch 1] 15 times in ring, sl st to 3rd ch in t-ch, do not turnâ€”16 dc.
Rnd 2: Sl st in ch-1 sp, ch 2, 2 dc-cl in same ch-1 sp, ch 3, *3 dc-cl in next ch-1 sp, ch 5, [3 dc-cl in next ch-1 sp, ch 3] 3 times; rep from * around to last 3 ch-1 sps, 3 dc-cl in next ch-1 sp, ch 5, 3 dc-cl in next ch-1 sp, ch 3, 3 dc-cl in next ch-1 sp, ch 1, hdc in top of 2 dc-cl, do not turnâ€”16 dc-cl.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, *ch 5, sc in next ch-3 sp, [5 dc, ch 5, 5 dc] in next ch-5 sp, sc in next ch-3 sp, ch 5, sc in next ch-3 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”12 ch-sps.

stitches.description: This fun motif mimics a sunflower with clusters that are turned on their sides to encircle the center.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 7, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 12 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”12 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 2, dc in next sc, ch 3 (counts as dc2tog), *dc2tog in same sc and next sc, ch 3; rep from * 9 times, dc2tog in same sc and next sc, ch 1, hdc in top of 1st dc, do not turnâ€”12 dc2tog.
Rnd 3: Ch 5 (counts as hdc), 2dc-cl in 3rd ch from hk, *hdc in next ch-3 sp, ch 3, 2dc-cl in 3rd ch from hk; rep from * around, sl st to 2nd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 dc-cl.
Rnd 4: Ch 6 (counts as hdc, ch-4 sp), *hdc in next hdc, ch 4; rep from * around, sl st to 2nd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 hdc.
Rnd 5: Sl st to ch-4 sp, ch 3 (counts as dc), [4 dc, ch 5, 5 dc] in same ch-4 sp, *sc in next ch-4 sp, ch 5, sc in next ch-4 sp, [5 dc, ch 5, 5 dc] in next ch-4 sp; rep from * twice, sc in next ch-4 sp, ch 5, sc in next ch-4 sp, sl st to t-ch, fasten off.

stitches.description: The Y-stitches in this motif bring it to life in the voids they leave behind, almost like the lead in stained glass.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, sc in ring 24 times, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”24 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 6, *[sk next sc, y-st in next sc] twice, sk next sc, dtr in next sc, ch 5, dc in center of post of prev dtr; rep from * twice, [sk next sc, y-st in next sc] twice, dc in 3rd ch of t-ch, ch 2, dc in 6th ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 5 (counts as tr, ch-1 sp), [dc, ch 1, hdc, sc] around post of dc, *[sc, hdc, (ch 1, dc) twice, ch 1, hdc, sc] in next 2 ch-3 sps, [sc, hdc, ch 1, dc, (ch 1, tr) twice, ch 1, dc, ch 1, hdc, sc] in next ch-5 sp; rep from * twice, [sc, hdc, (ch 1, dc) twice, ch 1, hdc, sc] in next 2 ch-3 sps, [sc, hdc, ch 1, dc, ch 1, tr, ch 1] in ch-2 sp, sl st to 4th ch of t-ch, fasten off.

stitches.description: A large flower blooms out of the center of this granny square, and the color changes highlight the dramatic bloom.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 18 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”18 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, *sc in next sc, ch 3, sk 2 sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, [sc, hdc, 2 dc, sc] in ea ch-3 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 petals.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, *sc btw 2 sc, ch 3, sk 1 ch; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 ch-sps.
Rnd 5: [Sc, hdc, 5 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-3 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 ch-sps.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, *sc btw 2 sc, ch 3, sk 1 ch; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 ch-sps.
Rnd 7: [Sc, hdc, 5 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-6 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 ch-sps.
Rnd 8: Ch 1, *sc btw 2 sc, ch 3, sk 1 ch; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off, do not turnâ€”6 ch-sps.
Rnd 9: Ch 5 sp with at sl st, ch 1, sc in next ch-5 sp with at sl st, ch 3 (counts as dc),(7 dc, ch 2, 7 dc) in next ch-8 sp, ch 3, dc in next ch-5 sp, ch 3; rep from * around to last 2 ch-5 sps, [dc, ch 2, dc] in next ch-8 sp, ch 3, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”76 dc.
Rnd 10: Ch 3 (counts as dc), *dc in ea dc to ch-2 sp, [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in ch-2 sp; rep from * around to last to last dc, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turn.
Rnd 11: Join new color with sl st to next dc, *sc in next dc, ch 5, [sc, ch 5, sc] in next ch-2 sp, ch 5, sc in next dc, ch 5; rep from * around to last 2 ch-5 sps, [ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp] twice, ch 5, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”24 ch-sps.
Rnd 12: Ch 1, sc around post of dc, *[ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp] twice, ch 5, [sc, ch 5, sc] in next ch-5 sp] 3 times; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”28 ch-sps.

stitches.description: The allure of this motif is all in the flower. Crocheted all in one color, this sculptural flower seamlessly lies down in the background of the motif.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 5 (counts as dc, ch-2 sp), [dc, ch 2] 7 times in ring, sl st to 3rd ch on t-ch, do not turnâ€”8 dc.
Rnd 2: [Sc, hdc, 3 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-2 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 petals.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, *sc btw 2 sc on prev rnd, ch 4, sk petal; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-sps.
Rnd 4: [Sc, hdc, 5 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-4 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-sps.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, *sc btw 2 sc on prev rnd, ch 5, sk petal; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-sps.
Rnd 6: [Sc, hdc, 7 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-5 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off, do not turnâ€”8 petals.
Rnd 7: Sl st to ch-5 sp, [4 dc, ch 3, 4 dc] in ea ch-5 sp across, *ch 5, sc in center dc of next petal, picot, ch 5, sc in center dc of next petal, ch 5, [4 dc, ch 3, 4 dc] in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 ch-sps.
Rnd 8: Ch 3 (counts as dc), *dc in ea dc to ch-2 sp, [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in next ch-2 sp; rep from * around to last to dc in last 2 ch-3 sp, ch 5, 3 dc in ea 4 tr-cl to ch-2 sp, dc in next sc; rep from * around to last to top of t-ch, fasten offâ€”108 dc.

stitches.description: This motif mimics Gothic stained glass when combined with a larger fabric.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 8, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, *sc in ring, ch 3, 2 tr-cl in ring, ch 3; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 7 (counts as dtr, ch-2 sp), *[dc, ch 5, dc] in tr-cl, ch 2, dtr in next sc, ch 5, dc in center of prev dtr, ch 2; rep from * 3 times, [dc, ch 5, dc] in tr-cl, ch 2, dc in 3rd ch of t-ch, ch 2, dc in 5th ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”16 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 6 (counts as dc, ch-3 sp), *[(tr, ch 1) twice, tr, ch 3, (tr, ch 1) twice, tr] in ch-5 sp, ch 3, dc in next ch-5 sp, ch 3; rep from * to last ch-5 sp, *[(tr, ch 1) twice, tr, ch 3, (tr, ch 1) twice, tr] in last ch-5 sp, ch 3, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”28 ch-sps.
Rnd 4: Ch 6 (count as dc, ch-3 sp), *dc in next tr, ch 4, [3 dc, ch 3, 3 dc] in ch-3 sp, ch 4, sk 2 tr, dc in next tr, ch 3, dc in dc; rep from * around, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, fasten offâ€”20 ch-sps.

stitches.description: The lacy fan in each corner of this motif creates a stunning, lacy design.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), 3 dc in ring, [ch 2, 4 dc] 3 times in ring, hdc in top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”16 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc around post of hdc, *dc in next 2 dc, ch 7, dc in next 2 dc, [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in ch-2 sp; rep from * around to last ch-2 sp, dc in next 2 dc, ch 7, dc in next 2 dc, 2 dc around post of hdc, hdc in top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”32 dc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, *ch 4, 7 dc in ch-7 sp, ch 4, sc in ch-2 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”28 dc.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, *sc in sc, ch 6, [dc, ch 1] in next 6 dc, dc in next dc, ch 6; rep from * around to last sc, sc in last sc, ch 6, [dc, ch 1] in next 6 dc, dc in next dc, ch 3, dc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”28 dc.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, sc around post of dc, *ch 3, sc in next ch-6 sp, ch 3, [dc in next dc, picot, 2 dc in next ch-1 sp] 6 times, dc in next dc, picot, ch 3, sc in next ch-6 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”76 dc.

stitches.description: The celestial design in this lacy motif makes for lovely shawls and tunics.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), 2 dc in ring, [ch 9, 3 dc] 7 times in ring, ch 4, dtr in top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”24 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc around post of dtr, *ch 6, sk 1 dc, sc in next dc, ch 6, sc in ch-9 sp; rep from * around to last ch-9 sp, ch 6, sk 1 dc, sc in next dc, ch 4, hdc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, *[ch 5, sc in next ch-6 sp] twice, ch 11, sc in next ch-6 sp, ch 5, sc in next ch-6 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 ch-sps.
Rnd 4: Sl st to ch-5 sp, ch 4 (counts as tr), 6 tr in same ch-5 sp, *ch 5, [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc, ch 5, 2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in ch-11 sp, ch 5, sk ch-5 sp, 7 tr in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”28 tr.
Rnd 5: Ch 5 (counts as tr, ch-1 sp), *[tr, ch 1] in ea tr to last tr, tr in last tr, ch 6, sk 1 ch-5 sp, [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc, ch 5, 2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in next ch-5 sp, ch 6; rep from * around, sl st to 4th ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”44 ch-sps.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, *[sc, ch 3] in ea ch-1 sp across, ch 7, sk ch-6 sp, [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc, ch 5, 2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in next ch-5 sp, ch 7; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description: This large motif has lovely lace that transforms from a circle to a square.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 7, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 4 (counts as dc, ch-1 sp), [dc, ch 1] 15 times in ring, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”16 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, [sc, ch 3] in ea ch-1 sp around to last ch-1 sp, sc in ch-1 sp, ch 1, hdc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 sc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, [ch 4, sc] in ea ch-3 sp around, ch 2, hdc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 ch-sps.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, [ch 5, sc] in ea ch-4 sp around, ch 2, dc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 ch-sps.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, sc around post of dc, *[ch 1, dc in ch-5 sp] 8 times, ch 1, sc in next ch-5 sp, [ch 1, dc in next ch-5 sp] 8 times twice, ch 1, sc in next ch-5 sp, ch 1, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around to last 2 ch-5 sps, [ch 1, dc in next ch-5 sp] 8 times, ch 1, sc in next ch-5 sp, do not turnâ€”44 ch-sps.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, *[ch 2, dc in next ch-2 sp] 8 times total, ch 2, sc in next ch-5 sp, ch 5; rep from * around to last 2 ch-5 sps, [ch 2, dc in next ch-5 sp] twice, ch 2, dc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”40 ch-sps.
Rnd 7: Ch 1, sc around post of dc, *[ch 3, dc in dc] 3 times, ch 3, sk 1 ch-2 sp, ch 3, [3 dc in dc] 3 times, ch 3, sc in next ch-1-2 sp, [ch 3, dc in dc] 3 times, ch 3, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”36 ch-sps.

stitches.description: You can transform this exquisite design into an amazing fabric when you connect motifs at the corner chain spaces.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 4, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, [sc, ch 3, tr] into ring 4 times, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”4 tr.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc in 1st sc, *sc in next ch, tr, ch 4, dc in next sc, dc in next sc, ch 4, sl st in last tr, ch 4, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”8 ch-4 sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 3 (counts as dc), same sl st, *sc in next ch-4 sp, ch 1, [2 tr, ch 2, 2 tr] in next sc, ch 1, sc in next ch-4 sp, ch 3, dc in next sc, [2 tr, ch 2, 2 tr] in next sc; rep from * around to last dc, sc in next ch-4 sp, ch 1, [2 tr, ch 2, 2 tr] in next sc, ch 1, sc in last ch-4 sp on prev rnd, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 ch-sps.
Rnd 4: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next 2 tr, *ch 5, [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in ch-2 sp, ch 5, dc in tr, dc in next 2 dc, dc in tr; rep from * around, 3 dc in ch-sp, dc in last tr, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”56 ac.
Rnd 5: Ch 5 (counts as dc, ch-2 sp), sk 1 dc, *dc in next sc, ch 2, sk 1 dc, hdc in next sc, ch 2, hdc in next sc [sk 1 sc, sc in next sc, ch 1, hdc in next sc] twice, ch 5, sc, ch 5, sc] in next ch-2 sp, [ch 5, sc] twice in next ch-2 sp, ch 5, sc in 3rd ch of t-ch of next dc, ch 2, dc in next sc sc; rep from * around, sl st in 3rd ch of t-ch, fasten off.

stitches.description: The picots in this motif draw the eye to the blossom in the center. When combined, the chain spaces on the last round look like railroad ties.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, sc 24 times in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”24 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 2, 2 dc-cl in 1st sc, *[ch 3, sk 1 sc, 3 dc-cl in next sc] twice, ch 5, sk 1 sc, 3 dc-cl in next sc; rep from * twice more, [ch 3, sk 1 sc, 3 dc-cl in next sc] twice, ch 2, dc in top of 2 dc-cl, do not turnâ€”12 dc-cl.
Rnd 3: Ch 5 (counts as tr, ch-sp), [tr, picot, tr, ch 1, tr, picot, tr] around post of dc, *ch 2, sk 1 dc-cl, sc in next dc-cl, ch 2, [(tr, picot, tr, ch 1) 4 times, tr, picot, tr] in next ch-5 sp; rep from * twice, ch 2, sk 1 dc-cl, sc in next dc-cl, ch 2, [(tr, picot, tr, ch 1) twice, tr, picot] in next ch-2 sp, sl st to 4th ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”40 tr.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc in next ch, ch 5, sk (tr, picot, tr), sc in next ch-1 sp, *ch 5, sk (tr, picot, tr), dc2tog in next 2 ch-2 sps, [ch 5, sk (tr, picot, tr), sc in next ch-1 sp] 4 times; rep from * twice, ch 5, sk (tr, picot, tr), dc2tog in next 2 ch-2 sps, [ch 5, sk (tr, picot, tr) sc in next ch-1 sp] twice, ch 2, dc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”20 ch-sps.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, 3 sc around post of dc, [3 sc, ch 3, 3 sc] in ea ch-5 sp around, 3 sc in last ch-sp, ch 3, sl st in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”120 sc.

Ch 3 (counts as dc), 15 dc in ring, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turn, pull ring closedâ€”16 dc.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, sc in top of t-ch, sc in next 3 dc, *ch 3, sc in next 4 dc; rep from * around, ch 1, hdc to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 5 (counts as dc, ch-2 sp), 2 dc around post of hdc, *ch 4, sk next sc, sc in next 2 sc, ch 4, sk next sc, 2 sc, ch 4, sk next sc in ch-1 sp, sl to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”8 sc.
Rnd 3: Ch 5 (counts as dc, ch-2 sp), 2 dc in next ch-2 sp, *ch 3, 11 dc in ch-5 sp, ch 3, [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in next ch-2 sp; rep from * twice, ch 3, 11 dc in ch-5 sp, ch 3, [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in next ch-2 sp, sl st in 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”60 dc.
Rnd 4: Ch 5 (counts as dc, ch-2 sp), 2 dc in ch-2 sp, *ch 3, [hdc, ch 1] in last dc and ea dc across to last dc, hdc in last dc, ch 3, [2 dc, ch 1, 2 dc] in next ch-2 sp; rep from * around, ch 3, [hdc, ch 1] in last dc and ea dc across to last dc, hdc in last dc, ch 3, dc in last ch-2 sp, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”44 hdc.
Rnd 5: Ch 6 (counts as dc, ch-2 sp), 2 dc in ch-2 sp, *ch 3, [hdc, ch 1] in last dc and ea dc across to last dc, hdc in last dc, ch 3, [2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc] in next ch-2 sp; rep from * twice, ch 3, [hdc, ch 1] in last dc and ea dc across to last dc, hdc in last dc, ch 3, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”44 hdc.
Rnd 6: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in ch-2 sp, *ch 3, [hdc, ch 1] in next 5 ch-1 sps, sc in next ch-1 sp, ch 5, sc in ch-6 sp, ch 5, picot, 3 dc in ch-1 sp; rep from * around to last ch-1 sp, picot, ch 5, sc in next ch-6 sp, ch 5, picot, dc in last ch-1 sp, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten off.
Rnd 7: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in ch-2 sp, *ch 2, dc in ch-2 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”44 hdc.
Rnd 8: Ch 1, sc in ch-2 sp, *ch 4, [sc in next 2 ch-1 sps, ch 3, sc] in next 2 ch-1 sps, ch 4, sc in ch-2 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”40 sc.

stitches.description: This simple little flower makes a great appliquÃ© around the neckline of a girl's top.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 5, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 7 (counts as dc, ch-4 sp), [2 dc in ring, ch 4] 5 times, dc in ring, sl st in 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 dc.
Rnd 2: [Sl st, sc, hdc, 5 dc, hdc, sc, sl st] in ea ch-sp around, fasten offâ€”30 dc.

stitches.description: The small flower makes a great motif to fill in spaces between larger flower motifs.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 5, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, [sc, ch 7, sc] 4 times in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 sc.
Rnd 2: [Sc, 4 dc, ch 3, 4 dc, sc] in ea ch-sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”32 dc.

stitches.description: Classically simple in design, this motif makes a great accessory (scarf or headband) when combining multiple motifs together.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 5, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), 2 dc in ring, [ch 5, 3 dc] 4 times in ring, ch 5, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”15 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 3 (counts as dc), [(dc, ch 1) 6 times, dc] in next 4 ch-5 sps, [(dc, ch 1) 6 times] in last ch-5 sp; sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”35 dc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, *[sc, ch 3] in next 5 ch-1 sps, sc in next ch-1 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”30 sc.

stitches.description: When made in thread and stiffened, this motif can be a fun earring.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 8, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 16 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 7 (counts as dc, ch-4 sp), *sk 1 sc, dc in next sc, ch 4; rep from * around, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”8 dc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, [sc, hdc, 2 dc, ch 3, 2 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”32 dc.

stitches.description: The voids made by the chain spaces create a visible cross that you can use as a design feature when you combine them all together.
stitches.instructions:
Trefoil: Ch 4, sl st in 4th ch, ch 6, sl st in 6th ch, ch 4, sl st in 4th ch.
Picot: Ch 4, sl st in 4th ch.
Make an adjustable ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 4 (counts as dc, ch 1), [3 dc, trefoil, 3 dc, ch 1] 3 times, 3 dc, trefoil, 2 dc] in ring, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, pull adjustable ring closed, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in ch-1 sp, *picot, ch 5, sc in ch-6 sp, ch 5, picot, 3 dc in ch-1 sp; rep from * around to last ch-1 sp, picot, ch 5, sc in next ch-6 sp, ch 5, picot, dc in last ch-1 sp, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten off.

stitches.description: The back and forth of lace and solid rounds makes this flower great in a larger lace or solid project.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), 23 dc in ring, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”24 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc in top of t-ch, *ch 2, sk next dc, sc in next dc, ch 3, sk next dc, sc in next dc; rep from * around to last 3 dc, ch 2, sk next dc, sc in next dc, ch 1, hdc in 1st sc (counts as ch-3 sp), do not turnâ€”12 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 4 (counts as dc, ch-1 sp), 3 dc around post of hdc, *sc in next ch-2 sp, [3 dc, ch 1, 3 dc] in next ch-3 sp; rep from * around, sc in last ch-2 sp, 2 dc in 1st ch-sp, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”6 sc.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, *sc in ch-1 sp, ch 4, sk next dc, sc in next dc, ch 5, sk next [dc, sc, dc], sc in next dc, ch 4; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”18 ch-sps.

stitches.description: The chain spaces on the last round make a perfect place to connect larger motifs together, while filling the fabric with adorable little flowers.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 4, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, [sc, ch 4, sc, ch 3] 4 times in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-sps.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, *[sc, ch 5, hdc, ch 5, sc] in next ch-4 sp, [sc, ch 5, hdc, ch 5, sc] in next ch-3 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”12 ch-sps.

stitches.description: This leafy motif can make a great base for a sculptural flower, or when combining motifs together, makes a great tablecloth fabric.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 7, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 2, 2 dc-cl in ring, ch 3, *3 dc-cl in ring, ch 5, 3 dc-cl in ring, ch 3; rep from * twice, 3 dc-cl in ring, ch 2, dc in top of 2 dc-cl, do not turnâ€”seven 3 dc-cl.
Rnd 2: Ch 3, 2 tr-cl around post of prev rnd's dc, ch 4, 3 dc-cl around post of dc, ch 3, *sc in next ch-3 sp, ch 3, [3 dc-cl, ch 4, 3 tr-cl, ch 4, 3 dc-cl] in next ch-5 sp, ch 3; rep from * around, sc in last ch-3 sp, ch 3, 3 dc-cl in ch-2 sp, ch 4, sl st to top of 2 tr-cl, do not turnâ€”eight 3 dc-cl.
Rnd 3: Ch 3 (counts as dc), *[dc, 3 hdc] in next ch-4 sp, 2 sc in next ch-3 sp, sl st to sc, 2 sc in next ch-3 sp, [3 hdc, dc] in next ch-4 sp, dc in 3 tr-cl; rep from * around, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 dc.
Rnd 4: [Sc, ch 3, sc, ch 5, sc, ch 3, sc] in top of t-ch, *ch 2, sc in next dc, [ch 2, sc in next hdc] 3 times, ch 2, sl st in next 2 sc, sk sl st, sl st in next 2 sc, [ch 2, sc in next hdc] 3 times, ch 2, sc in next dc, ch 2, [sc, ch 3, sc, ch 5, sc, ch 3, sc] in next dc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description: By slightly changing where you crochet the double crochet stitches on round 3, you can create a textured flower.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 12 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, *sc in next sc, ch 3, sk next sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 sc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, [sc in next sc, ch 3 (push ch forward and work behind ch), ch 2] 5 times, sc in next sc, ch 2, 3 dc in next sc on rnd 1 behind ch-3 sp, hdc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”18 dc.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc around hdc post, *ch 3, sc in next ch-2 sp; rep from * around, ch 3, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”12 ch-3 sps.

stitches.description: The three-dimensional nature of this motif is stunning on its own as a broach or ring.
stitches.instructions:
Picot: Ch 3, sl st to 1st ch.
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 24 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, *sc in next sc, ch 3, sk 2 sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-3 sps.
Rnd 3: [Sc, ch 2, 4 dc, ch 2, sc] in ea ch-3 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 petals.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, *sc btw next 2 sc, ch 5; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-5 sps.
Rnd 5: [Sc, ch 2, 6 dc, ch 2, sc] in ea ch-5 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 petals.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, *sc btw next 2 sc, ch 7; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-7 sps.
Rnd 7: [Sc, ch 2, 8 dc, ch 2, sc] in ea ch-7 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 petals.
Rnd 8: Ch 1, *sc btw next 2 sc, ch 9; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-9 sps.
Rnd 9: [Sc, ch 2, (dc, picot) 5 times, dc, ch 2, sc] in ea ch-9 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”8 petals.

stitches.description: Contrasting colors highlight the sculptural nature of this motif.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 8, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), 23 dc in ring, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”24 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc in top of t-ch, *ch 3, sk 1 dc, dc in next dc; rep from * around to last 2 dc, ch 1, hdc in 1st sc (counts as ch-3 sp), do not turnâ€”12 ch-3 sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, *7 dc in next ch-3 sp, sc in next ch-3 sp; rep from * around to last ch-3 sp, 7 dc in last ch-3 sp, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”6 petals.
Rnd 4: Join new color to next sc on rnd 2, sc in same sc, *ch 5, sc in next sc on rnd 2 (push rnd 3 forward and work behind rnd), ch 3, sc in next sc on rnd 2; rep from * around to last 2 sc, ch 5, sc in next sc on rnd 2, ch 1, hdc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”12 sc.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, *[6 dc, ch 2, 6 dc] in next ch-5 sp, sc in next ch-3 sp; rep from * around to last ch-3 sp, [6 dc, ch 2, 6 dc] in last ch-5 sp, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”6 petals.

stitches.description: These simple flowers make great decorations to add to kids' projects.
stitches.instructions:
Picot: Ch 4, sl st to 1st ch.
Ch 8, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), 17 dc in ring, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turn, fasten off colorâ€”18 dc.
Rnd 2: Join new color, *ch 6, sl st in next dc, picot, 3 tr in next dc, ch 6, sl st in next dc; rep from * around, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten offâ€”36 tr.

stitches.description: You can combine these realistic flowers for a brilliant purse or ottoman.
stitches.instructions:
Popcorn (pop): 5 dc in st indicated, remove hk, insert hk into 1st dc, place free lp on hk and pull through dc.
Beginning Popcorn (beg pop): 4 dc in st indicated, remove hk, insert hk into 1st dc, place free lp on hk and pull through dc.
Make an adjustable ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 6 sc in ring, pull ring closed, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 3, beg pop in 1st sc, [ch 3, pop in next sc] 5 times, ch 3, sl st to beg pop, fasten offâ€”6 pop.
Rnd 3: Join new color to beg pop, [sc, hdc, 3 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-3 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 petals.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc in same st, ch 5, *sc btw next 2 sc, ch 5; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”6 ch-5 sps.
Rnd 5: Join new color, [sc, hdc, 5 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-5 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”6 petals.

stitches.description: The simple chain spaces create a unique texture that mimics sunflowers.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 5, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, [sc, ch 5] 7 times in ring, sc in ring, ch 2, dc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc around post of dc, *ch 5, sk next 2 ch-5 sps, ch 5; rep from * around to last ch-5 sp, ch 5, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 ch-5 sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 7 (counts as dc, ch-4 sp), *sk next 2 ch-5 sps, *sk next 2 ch-5 sps, [hdc, ch 2] 5 times, sc in next sc, ch 5; rep from * around, [hdc, ch 2] 5 times, sc in next sc, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”40 ch-4 sps.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc in t-ch, *ch 6, sk 5 ch-4 sps, sc in next sc (push ch-sps forward, sk behind them); rep from * around to last 5 sc, ch 6, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-6 sps.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, sc in t-ch, *ch 6, sk 5 ch-4 sps, sc in next sc (push ch-sps forward, sk behind them); rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-7 sps.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, in t-ch, *[(ch, hdc) 4 ch-4 sps, sl st] in ch-6 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”48 ch-sps.
Rnd 7: Join new color to beg sc, *ch 7, sl st sc in next sc; rep from * around to last 6 sc, ch 7, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-7 sps.
Rnd 8: [Sc, hdc, 2 dc, 5 tr, 2 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-7 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”8 petals.

stitches.description: The popcorns burst from this motif to make eye-catching flowers that you can use as fun dish scrubbies.
stitches.instructions:
Popcorn (pop): 5 dc in st indicated, remove hk, insert hk into 1st dc, place free lp on hk and pull through dc.
Beginning Popcorn (beg pop): 4 dc in st indicated, remove hk, insert hk into 1st dc, place free lp on hk and pull through dc.
Make an adjustable ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 6 sc in ring, pull ring closed, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 3, beg pop in 1st sc, [ch 3, pop in next sc] 5 times, ch 3, sl st to beg pop, do not turnâ€”6 pop.
Rnd 3: Ch 3, beg pop in prev ch-sp, [(ch 3, pop) twice] in ea ch-3 sp around to last ch-sp, ch 3, pop in last ch-sp, ch 3, sl st in beg pop, do not turnâ€”12 pop.
Rnd 4: Ch 3, beg pop in prev ch-sp, [(ch 3, pop) twice] in ea ch-3 sp around to last ch-sp, ch 3, pop in last ch-sp, ch 1, hdc in beg pop, do not turnâ€”24 pop.
Rnd 5: Ch 3 (counts as dc), [dc, hdc, sc] around post of hdc, [sc, hdc, 3 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-3 sp around, [sc, hdc, dc] in 1st ch-1 sp, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten off.

stitches.description: Bursting with joy, this flower looks equally lovely in a large bouquet or as a simple cummerbund.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 32.
Row 1 (RS): Sl st in 2nd ch from hk, [ch 4, sk 2 dc, ch, sc in next ch] 6 times, *ch 3, sk 1 ch, sc in next ch; rep from * to end, turnâ€”12 ch-sps.
Row 2: Ch 1, [sc, ch 2, 4 hdc, 2 sc] in ea ch-3 sp, [sc, ch 3, 6 dc, ch 3, sc] in ea ch-4 sp across, turn.
Row 3: *Sl st in sc, sc in next sc, ch 6, sc in same sc, ch 4, sk btw 3rd and 4th dc, ch 3, sl st in same sc, ch 6, sl st in next sc; rep from * in over all large petals, *sl st in next sc, sc in next sc, ch 4, sk btw 2nd and 3rd hdc, ch 3, sl st in same sc, ch 4, sl st in next sc; rep from ** over all small petals, sl st to end with long tail.
Twist the flower into a rose by wrapping the large petals around the smaller petals, and securing them in the center.

stitches.description: Mums are one of the most unique flowers. The mums in this pattern work best with stiff acrylic or single-pile wool to let the petals curl. If your petals do not curl enough, try dropping down a few hook sizes before changing your yarn choice.
stitches.instructions:
Make an adjustable ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 2 (counts as hdc), 11 hdc in ring, pull ring closed, sl st to t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 hdc.
Rnd 2: *Ch 6, hdc in 3rd ch from hk and ea ch across (petal formed), sl st blp in next hdc; rep from * around, do not turnâ€”12 large petals.
Rnd 3: Sl st flp to t-ch on rnd 1, *ch 4, sc in 2nd ch from hk and ea ch across (petal formed), sl st flp in next hdc on rnd 1; rep from * around, fasten offâ€”12 small petals.

stitches.description: Crocheted one petal at a time, the motif spirals around itself into a Ferris wheel of a flower.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 8, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 16 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Petal 1: Ch 15, sl st in next sc, ch 3 (counts as dc), turn to work in ch-sp, 5 dc in ch-15 sp, ch 3 (counts as dc), sl st in 4th ch from rnd 1, sl st in next 5 ch, ch 3 (counts as dc), turn, 8 dc in ch-15 sp (around all sts), sl st to top of ch 3, do not turnâ€”16 dc.
Petal 2: Ch 9, sk next sc on rnd 1, sl st in next sc on rnd 1, ch 3 (counts as dc), turn to work in ch-sp, 5 dc in ch-9 sp, ch 3 (counts as dc), sl st in 4th ch from rnd 1, sl st in next 5 ch, ch 3 (counts as dc), turn, 8 dc in ch-9 sp, sl st to top of ch 3, do not turnâ€”16 dc.
Petals 3â€“8: Rep petal 2, sl st to petal 1, fasten off.

stitches.description: When left unblocked, this flower curls into a pretty daisy.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 5, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, [sc in ring, ch 6] 6 times, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”6 sc.
Rnd 2: [Sc, hdc, 5 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-6 sp around, do not turnâ€”6 petals.
Rnd 3: *Sl st to next sc, sc in next hdc, ch 1, hdc in next dc, [ch 1, dc in next dc] 3 times, ch 1, hdc in next dc, ch 1, sc in next hdc, sl st in next sc; rep from * around, do not turnâ€”6 petals.
Rnd 4: *Sl st to next sc, sc in next ch-1 sp, [sc, ch 3, sl st] in next 4 ch-1 sps, sc in next ch-1 sp, sl st to next sc; rep from * around, fasten offâ€”24 ch-sps.

stitches.description: This can be both a flower motif and a tiny doily. Stretch out and stiffen for a snowflake, or crochet tightly with a thick yarn to let the yarn curl the petals.
stitches.instructions:
Picot: Ch 3, sl st to 1st ch.
Ch 4, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 2, 2 dc-cl in ring, [ch 3, picot, 3 dc-cl in ring] 4 times, ch 3, picot, sl st to dc-cl, do not turnâ€”5 dc-cl.
Rnd 2: Sl st in ch-3 sp, ch 1, sc in same ch-3 sp, *ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * to last ch-5 sp, ch 2, dc in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”5 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Sl st around post of dc, ch 2, 2 dc-cl around post of dc, ch 2, sl st to next sc, *[ch 2, 2 dc-cl, ch 2, sl st, ch 2, 2 dc-cl, ch 2] in next ch-5 sp, sl st in next sc; rep from * around, ch 2, 2 dc-cl in 1st ch-2 sp, hdc in 1st sl st, do not turnâ€”10 dc-cl.
Rnd 4: Ch 3, sl st to hdc, *sl st to next ch-2 sp, ch 3, [sl st, ch 3, sl st] in next dc-cl, ch 3, sl st to next ch-2 sp; rep from * around to last ch-2 sp, sl st to last ch-2 sp, ch 3, [sl st, ch 3, sl st] in next dc-cl, fasten offâ€”30 ch-3 sps.

stitches.description: Easy repeating rounds make this doily burst from your hook with lightning speed.
stitches.instructions:
Picot: Ch 3, sl st to 1st sc.
Ch 12, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), 39 dc in ring, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”40 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next dc, *ch 3, dc in next 4 dc; rep from * around to last 2 dc, ch 3, dc in next 2 dc, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”10 ch-3 sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next dc, *ch 4, dc in next 4 dc; rep from * around to last 2 dc, ch 4, dc in next 2 dc, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”10 ch-4 sps.
Rnd 4: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next dc, *ch 5, dc in next 4 dc; rep from * around to last 2 dc, ch 5, dc in next 2 dc, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”10 ch-5 sps.
Rnd 5: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next dc, *ch 7, dc in next 4 dc; rep from * around to last 2 dc, ch 7, dc in next 2 dc, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”10 ch-7 sps.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, sc in top of t-ch, *[2 dc, (picot, 3 dc) twice, picot, 2 dc] in next ch-7 sp, sk next dc, sc in next 2 dc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”30 picots.

stitches.description: The tiny groups of chains on the final round of the doily make it ruffle ever so slightly for a feminine look.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 16 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 5 (counts as tr, ch-1 sp), *tr in next sc, ch 1; rep from * around, sl st in 4th ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”16 tr.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, *2 sc in ch-1 sp, ch 1, sk next tr; rep from * around to t-ch, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”32 sc.
Rnd 4: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next sc, ch 1, sk next ch-1 sp, *dc in next 2 sc, ch 1, sk next ch-1 sp; rep from * around, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”32 dc.
Rnd 5: Ch 7 (counts as dc, ch-4 sp), sk next ch-1 sp, dc in next ch-1 sp, ch 3, dc in skipped ch-1 sp (by inserting hk behind prev dc made), *ch 4, dc in next ch-1 sp, ch 3, dc in prev ch-1 sp (by inserting hk behind prev dc made); rep from * around, ch 4, dc in 1st ch-1 sp, ch 3, sl st in 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”32 ch-sps.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, *[(sc, ch 3) 3 times, sc] in next ch-4 sp, 2 sc in next ch-3 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”96 sc.

stitches.description: If you have not tried crocheting into the post of a stitch to see the cool shapes it makes, this doily will give you all the practice you need to master the technique.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 24 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”24 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 6, *sk next sc, dtr in next sc, ch 4, dc in middle of dtr post; rep from * around, dc in 3rd ch of t-ch, ch 2, hdc in 6th ch of t-ch (counts as ch-sp), do not turnâ€”12 ch-4 sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, 3 sc around post of hdc, *ch 3, 5 sc in next ch-4 sp; rep from * around, ch 3, 2 sc in ch-2 sp, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”60 sc.
Rnd 4: Ch 6, *dtr in next ch-3 sp, ch 4, dc in middle of dtr post, sk 2 sc, dtr in next sc, ch 4, dc in middle of dtr post; rep from * around, dtr in next ch-3 sp, ch 4, dc in middle of dtr post, dc in 3rd ch of t-ch, ch 2, hdc in 6th ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”23 ch-4 sps.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, 3 sc around post of hdc, *[sc, 4 hdc, sc] in next ch-4 sp, [3 sc, ch 2, 3 sc] in next ch-4 sp; rep from * around, [sc, 4 hdc, sc] in last ch-4 sp, 3 sc in ch-2 sp, ch 2, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”96 sc.

stitches.description: Simple and classic, this motif can be used to fill in spaces between larger, more intricate ones.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 8, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), 17 dc in ring, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”18 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 4 (counts as dc, ch-1 sp), [dc, ch 1] in ea dc around, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”18 dc.
Rnd 3: [Sc, ch 4, sc] in ea ch-1 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”18 ch-4 sps.

stitches.description: Ruffles and popcornâ€”what is not to love about this textured crazy motif? Use it alone in fun coasters or connected together in a shawl.
stitches.instructions:
Popcorn (pop): 5 dc in sp indicated and remove hk. Insert hk into top of 1st dc from front to back. Place free lp on hk and pull through 1st dc. Ch 1.
Ch 5, sl st to 1st ch to form ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 4 (counts as dc, ch-1 sp), [dc, ch 1] in ring 11 times, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 5 (counts as dc, ch-2 sp), [dc, ch 2] in ea dc around, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 dc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc in 3rd ch of t-ch, *ch 2, pop in next ch-2 sp, ch 2, sc to next dc; rep from * around to last dc, ch 2, pop in last ch-2 sp, ch 2, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”12 pop.
Rnd 4: *Ch 7, sk pop, sl st to next sc; rep from * around to beg sl st, sl st in sl st, do not turnâ€”12 ch-7 sps.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, [2 sc, 2 hdc, 6 dc, 2 hdc, 2 sc] in ea ch-7 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”12 petals.

stitches.description: The delicate picots are highlighted in the final solid round of this motif.
stitches.instructions:
Picot: Ch 4, sl st to 1st ch.
Ch 4, sl st to 1st ch to form ring.

stitches.description: Depending on your blocking technique, this motif can look very smooth and circular or spiky and ridged.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 5 (counts as dc, ch-2 sp), [dc, ch 2] 6 times in ring, dc in ring, hdc to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”8 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 3 (counts as dc), *[ch 1, picot] twice, ch 1, dc in next ch-2 sp; rep from * around to last ch-2 sp, [ch 1, picot] twice, ch 1, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, fasten offâ€”8 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Join yarn to ch-sp btw picots with sl st, sc in same sp, *ch 7, sc to next ch-sp btw picot; rep from * around, ending ch 7, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 sc.
Rnd 4: [Sc, hdc, 9 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-7 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”8 petals.
visual_cues: spiky or smooth depending on blocking

stitches.description: Bursting with chain-space petals, this motif makes for stunning jewelry when combined with simple beads.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 5, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 4 (counts as dc, ch-1 sp), [dc, ch 1] in ring 11 times, ch 1, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, [sc, ch 2, sc] in ea ch-1 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”24 sc.
Rnd 3: Ch 9 (counts as dc, ch-6 sp), sk sc and ch-2 sp, *dc btw next 2 sc, ch 6, sk ch-2 sp; rep from * around to last 3 sc, dc btw next 2 sc, ch 6, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 dc.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, [3 sc, ch 3, sc, ch 4, sc, ch 3, 3 sc] in ea ch-6 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description: This unassuming motif can make the most incredible fabric when you combine it multiple times in a sweater.
stitches.instructions:
Picot: Ch 3, sl st to 1st ch.
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 4 (counts as dc, ch-1), [3 dc, ch 1] into ring 7 times, 2 dc in ring, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”24 dc.
Rnd 2: Ch 2, [dc, ch 2, 2 dc-cl] in ch-1 sp, ch 5, *[2 dc-cl, ch 2, 2 dc-cl] in next ch-1 sp, ch 5; rep from * around to last ch-1 sp, [2 dc-cl, ch 2, 2 dc-cl] in last ch-1 sp, ch 2, dc to top of dc, do not turnâ€”15 dc-cl.
Rnd 3: Ch 3 (counts as dc), [2 dc, picot, 2 dc] around post of dc, *sc in next ch-2 sp, [2 dc, picot, 3 dc, ch 5, 3 dc, picot, 2 dc] in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around 6 more times, sc in next ch-2 sp, [2 dc, picot, 3 dc] in last ch-2 sp, ch 5, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten offâ€”80 dc.

stitches.description: Earthy in look, this motif works equally well in vests and tunics as it does in coasters and jewelry.
stitches.instructions:
Double Crochet Three Together (dc3tog): *Yo, insert hk into st indicated, yo, draw up lp, yo, draw through 2 lps on hk; rep from * twice in next st, yo, draw through last lps on hk.
Double Crochet Four Together (dc4tog): *Yo, insert hk into st indicated, yo, draw up lp, yo, draw through 2 lps on hk; rep from * once in same st; rep from * twice in next st, yo, draw through last lps on hk.
Ch 4, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 8 sc in ring, sl st to sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 2, dc3tog working 1st leg in 1st sc and next two legs in next sc, ch 4, [dc4tog in prev sc and next sc, ch 4] 7 times, sl st to top of dc3tog, do not turnâ€”8 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, *[3 sc, ch 4, 3 sc] in next ch-4 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-sps.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc between 1st and last sc from last rnd, ch 3, sc in next ch-4 sp, ch 3, *sk 2 sc, sc between next 2 sc, ch 3, sc in next ch-4 sp, ch 3; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, *4 sc in next ch-3 sp, ch 3, 4 sc in next ch-3 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.

stitches.description: If you are looking for a snowflake to mimic actual ones, this is for you. All the trefoils in the snowflake fan out to create a stunning motif.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 9, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 4 (counts as tr), 4 tr in ring, [ch 4, 5 tr in ring] 5 times, ch 4, sl st in top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”30 tr.
Rnd 2: Sl st in next 4 tr, *[sl st, ch 2, hdc, (ch 5, sl st to 1st ch) 3 times, sl st to prev hdc, hdc, ch 7, sl st in 3rd ch from hk, [ch 5, sl st to 1st ch] twice, sc in next 2 ch, hdc, [ch 5, sl st to 1st ch] 3 times, sl st to prev hdc, hdc, ch 2, sl st] in next ch-sp, sl st in next 5 tr; rep from * around, fasten offâ€”6 petals.

stitches.description: This flowery snowflake looks equally cute whether blocked and stiffened or unblocked and curly; try it each way to find your favorite look.
stitches.instructions:
Single Crochet Spike Stitch (spike st): Insert hk in next st 2 rnds below, yo, draw up a lp to height of current rnd, yo, pull through both lps on hk.
Picot: Ch 3, sl st to prev sc.
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 24 sc into ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, *sc in next 2 sc, [sc, ch 7, sc] in next sc, sc in next sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”30 sc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc in 1st sc, *[2 hdc, 13 dc, 2 hdc] in next ch-7 sp, sk 2 sc, sc in next sc; rep from * around to last ch-7 sp, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn, fasten off colorâ€”6 petals.
Rnd 4: Join new color, ch 1, *spike st in next sc on rnd 1, ch 3, sk 2 hdc and 2 dc, sc in next dc, picot, ch 3, sk 3 dc, sc in next dc, picot, ch 3, sk 3 dc, sc in next dc, picot, ch 3, sk 2 dc and 2 hdc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st spike st, fasten off.

stitches.description: Like a star that fell from heaven, this snowflake shines with the beauty of clusters and arches.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 7, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, [sc, ch 4] into ring 11 times, sc in ring, ch 2, hdc to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”12 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 2, 2 dc-cl around post of hdc, *ch 3, 3 dc-cl in next ch-4 sp; rep from * around, ch 3, sl st to 2 dc-cl, do not turnâ€”12 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, [3 sc, ch 3, 3 sc] in ea ch-3 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”12 ch-sps.
Rnd 4: Sl st in next 2 sc, sl st in ch-3 sp, ch 2, [2 dc-cl, ch 7, 3 dc-cl] in same ch 3-sp, *[3 dc-cl, ch 6, 3 dc-cl] in next ch-3 sp, [3 dc-cl, ch 7, 3 dc-cl] in next ch-3 sp; rep from * around, [3 dc-cl, ch 6, 3 dc-cl] in last ch-3 sp, sl st to 2 dc-cl, do not turnâ€”24 dc-cl.
Rnd 5: *[6 sc, ch 3, 6 sc] in next ch-7 sp, 11 sc in next ch-6 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”138 sc.

stitches.description: Delicate and lacy, this snowflake combines easily with itself to make a beautiful fabric that almost looks like lattice.
stitches.instructions:
X-Stitch (X-st): Yo 3 times, insert hk in next dc, yo and draw up lp, [yo, pull through 2 lps on hk] twice, sk 2 dc, yo, insert hk in next dc, yo and draw up lp, [yo, pull through 2 lps on hk] twice (dc made), [yo and pull through 2 lps] twice, ch 3, dc in top of dc just made.
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in ring, ch 3, [2 dc, ch 3] into ring 5 times, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 3, dc in t-ch, 2 dc in next dc, ch 4, sl st to 2nd ch, ch 1, *2 dc in next 2 dc, ch 4, sl st to 2nd ch, ch 1; rep from * around, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”24 dc.
Rnd 3: Ch 3, sk 2 dc, dc in next dc, ch 6, dc in prev dc, ch 6, sl st to 4th ch, ch 3, *sk ch-6 sp, X-st over next 4 dc, ch 6, sl st to 4th ch, ch 3; rep from * around to 3rd ch of 1st ch-6 sp, do not turn.
Rnd 4: Ch 2, [2 dc-cl, ch 3, 3 dc-cl, ch 5, 3 dc-cl, ch 3, 3 dc-cl] in ch-3 sp, ch 6, sl st to 4th ch, ch 3, *sk ch-6 sp, [3 dc-cl, ch 3, 3 dc-cl, ch 5, 3 dc-cl, ch 3, 3 dc-cl] in next ch-3 sp of X-st, ch 6, sl st to 4th ch, ch 3; rep from * around to 2 dc-cl, fasten off.

stitches.description: Part flower, part snowflakeâ€”this motif can stand in as either in your next crochet project.
stitches.instructions:
Trefoil: Ch 7, sl st in 7th ch, ch 9, sl st in 9th ch, ch 7, sl st in 7th ch.
Small Picot (sm picot): Ch 3, sl st in 3rd ch.
Large Picot (lg picot): Ch 5, sl st in 5th ch.
Ch 11, sl st to 1st ch to form ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 3 (counts as dc), 23 dc in ring, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”24 dc.
Rnd 2: *[Sl st, ch 1, sm picot, ch 1, lg picot, 2 tr, trefoil, 2 tr, lg picot, ch 1, sm picot, ch 1, sl st] in next st, sl st to next 3 dc; rep from * around, fasten offâ€”6 petals.

stitches.description: The numerous chain spaces on the last round of this snowflake give you multiple chances to connect it with other motifs or places to thread it onto an earring hook.
stitches.instructions:
Shell (sh): [Sc, hdc, 2 dc, ch 2, 2 dc, hdc, sc] in st indicated.
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 12 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”12 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 7 (counts as dc and ch 4), sk next sc, [dc in next sc, ch 4, sk 1 sc] 4 times, sk 1 sc, dc in next sc, ch 1, dc in 3rd ch of beg ch-7, do not turnâ€”6 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 5 (counts as dc and ch 2), [2 dc, hdc, sc] around post of dc, [sh in next 4-ch sp] 5 times, [sc, hdc, dc] in the next ch-1 sp, join with a sl st to 3rd ch of beg ch-5, do not turnâ€”6 sh.
Rnd 4: Ch 4 (counts as tr), 4 tr in 1st ch-2 sp, [9 tr in next ch-2 sp] 5 times, 4 tr in 1st ch-2 sp, join with a sl st to 4th ch of beg ch-4, do not turnâ€”54 tr.
Rnd 5: Ch 8 (counts as dc, ch-5 sp), 2 dc in top of t-ch, *ch 2, sk 2 tr, sc in next tr, ch 2, sk 1 tr, [dc, ch 3, dc] in sp btw 9 tr of prev rnd, ch 2, sk 1 tr, sc in next tr, ch 2, sk 2 tr, [2 dc, ch 5, 2 dc] in next tr; rep from * 4 times more, ch 2, sk 2 tr, sc in next tr, ch 2, sk 1 tr, [dc, ch 3, dc] in sp btw 9 tr of prev rnd, ch 2, sk 1 tr, sc in next tr, ch 2, dc in top of t-ch, sl st to 3rd ch of beg ch-8, fasten offâ€”36 ch-sps.

stitches.description: This star-like snowflake will seem far out after crocheting the first two rounds. You will have to stick with the directions to watch it transform into the beautiful motif it truly is.
stitches.instructions:
Trefoil: [Ch 7, sl st to 7th ch, ch 11, sl st to 11th ch, ch 7, sl st to 7th ch] in st indicated.
Ch 9, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 18 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”18 sc.
Rnd 2: Ch 6, sl st to same sc, *sl st to next sc, ch 6, [2 dc, ch 3, sl st] in 3rd ch, sc in next 2 ch, sl st in prev sc, [sl st, ch 3, dc] in next sc, [dc, ch 3, sl st] in next sc; rep from * around to last 2 sc, sl st to next sc, ch 6, [2 dc, ch 3, sl st] in 3rd ch, sc in next 2 ch, sl st in prev sc, [sl st, ch 3, dc] in last sc, sl st to 3rd ch of t-châ€”6 petals.
Rnd 3: *Ch 6, [sl st, trefoil, sl st] btw next 2 dc, ch 6, sl st btw next 2 dc; rep from * around, fasten offâ€”6 trefoils.

stitches.description: The lacy chain spaces of this snowflake's center set up the final round for the dramatic set of double crochet. Be sure to block to get the snowflake to lay flat.
stitches.instructions:
Picot: Ch 4, sl st to prev sc.
Ch 6, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 2, 2 dc-cl in ring, [ch 4, 3 dc-cl] in ring 5 times, ch 2, hdc in top of 2 dc-cl, do not turnâ€”6 dc-cl.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, *ch 5, sc in dc-cl, ch 5, sc in ch-4 sp; rep from * around to last ch-4 sp, ch 5, sc in dc-cl, ch 2, dc in 1st sc (counts as ch-sp), do not turnâ€”12 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc around post of dc, *ch 7, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around to last ch-5 sp, ch 3, tr in 1st sc, do not turnâ€”12 ch-sps.
Rnd 4: Ch 3 (counts as dc), 6 dc around post of dc, *sc in next ch-7 sp, picot, 13 dc in next ch-7 sp; rep from * around to last ch-7 sp, sc in last ch-7 sp, picot, 6 dc in ch-3 sp, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten off.

stitches.description: Moroccan inspired, this snowflake highlights trefoils on two rounds to draw the eye to their beauty.
stitches.instructions:
Trefoil: [Ch 3, sl st to 1st ch] 3 times.
Ch 10, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 4 (counts as tr), 23 tr in ring, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”24 tr.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc in top of t-ch, *ch 4, sk 2 tr, sc in next tr; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 ch-sps.
Rnd 3: [Sc, hdc, 2 dc, trefoil, sl st to prev dc, 2 dc, hdc, sc] in ea ch-4 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”8 petals.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc in sl st, *ch 6, sk 1 ch-3 sp, sc in next ch-3 sp, ch 6, sc btw next 2 sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”16 ch-sps.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, *7 sc in next ch-6 sp, trefoil, sl st to prev sc, 7 sc in next ch-6 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”8 trefoils.

stitches.description: The crossed double crochets in the snowflake add just a touch of texture to this classical design.
stitches.instructions:
Crossed Double Crochet (crossed dc): Sk next 2 sc, dc in next sc, ch 3, dc in prev 3rd sc (sk same 2 sc backward).
Ch 7, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 12 sc in ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 5 (counts as dc, ch-2 sp), *dc in next sc, ch 2; rep from * around, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 dc.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc on top of t-ch, *2 sc in ch-2 sp, sc in next dc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turnâ€”36 sc.
Rnd 4: Ch 6 (counts as dc, ch-3 sp), dc in prev 3rd sc (sk prev 2 sc), [ch 2, crossed dc] 11 times, ch 2, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, do not turnâ€”12 crossed dc.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, *[2 sc, ch 2, 2 sc] in ch-3 sp, 2 sc in next ch-2 sp, [sc, (ch 3, hdc) 3 times, ch 3, sc] in next ch-3 sp, 2 sc in next ch-2 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten offâ€”18 hdc.

stitches.description: This chunky snowflake is made more delicate with the tiny picots on the last rounds. The picots also make a great location to join other motifs to this one.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 7, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 2, 2 dc-cl in ring, [ch 3, 3 dc-cl] in ring 7 times, ch 1, hdc in top of 2 dc-cl, do not turnâ€”8 dc-cl.
Rnd 2: Ch 2 (counts as hdc), 2 hdc around post of hdc, *ch 5, 4 hdc in next ch-3 sp; rep from * around, ch 5, hdc in ch-1 sp, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turnâ€”32 hdc.
Rnd 3: Sl st in next hdc, *[3 hdc, ch 3, sl st in prev hdc, 2 hdc, ch 3, 3 hdc, ch 3, sl st in prev hdc, 2 hdc] in next ch-5 sp, sl st btw next 2nd and 3rd hdc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sl st, fasten offâ€”80 hdc.

stitches.description: The star shape to this simple snowflake makes it a standout in any project from shawl to scarf.
stitches.instructions:
Ch 7, sl st to 1st ch to form a ring.
Rnd 1 (RS): Ch 1, 12 sc into ring, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 2, 2 dc-cl in 1st sc, ch 3, *3 dc-cl in next sc, ch 5, 3 dc-cl in next sc, ch 3; rep from * around to last sc, 3 dc-cl in last sc, ch 2, dc in top of 2 dc-cl, do not turnâ€”12 dc-cl.
Rnd 3: Ch 3 (counts as dc), 4 dc around post of dc, *sc in next ch-3 sp, [5 dc, ch 3, 5 dc] in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around to last ch-3 sp, sc in last ch-3 sp, 5 dc in ch-2 sp, ch 3, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten offâ€”60 dc.

stitches.description:
This simple seam creates a little pucker that you can use as a design feature, or turn it upside down and make it invisible.

Pin motifs together with wrong sides together for a ridge seam, or right sides together for a hidden seam.

Join the yarn to the edge, slip stitch across each stitch in both motifs (inserting the hook through both motifs at the same time), and fasten off.

This seam uses the ridge to add a gentle wave between each motif.

Reverse Single Crochet (rev sc): Insert hk back into prev st (to the right in the clockwise direction), yo, pull up lp twisting hk around to face upwards, yo, pull through sts on hk.

Pin the motifs together with wrong sides together.

Join the yarn to the left edge, and reverse single crochet across each stitch in both motifs at the same time (inserting the hook through the back loop of the motif closest to you and the front loop of the motif farthest from you); working across to the right edge, fasten off.

This incredibly simple seam looks more complex, which makes the beauty of it sing. This motif looks great on motifs with solid final rounds or ones with a regular spacing of tall stitches, like granny squares.

Hold the motifs together with wrong sides facing.

Join the yarn to the edge of the motif closest to you. Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next st of motif closest to you, *sk 2 sts in motif farthest from you, dc in next 2 sts of far motif, sk 2 sts in near motif, dc in next 2 sts of near motif; rep from * across, fasten off.

This is one of the most common ways to join two lacy motifs. The chain spaces become invisible in the larger fabric.

Align the edges of the motifs side-by-side, and join the yarn to the motif on the right.

Ch 1, sc in same sp as join, *ch 3, sc in next ch-sp on motif on left, ch 3, sc in next ch-sp on motif on right; rep from * across, fasten off.

Less of a seam and more of a way just to join two motifs, you can crochet motifs together as you go, by replacing a chain with a slip stitch and literally crocheting one motif onto another.

Crochet the next motif to the last round.

Follow the directions for the last round of the motif. When you get to a chain space, remove the middle chain and replace it with a slip stitch to the same space on an adjoining motif. In the example shown, you would chain 2, slip stitch to the adjoining motif, chain 2, and then continue crocheting the working motif.

When motifs are connected and have a void, you can use small motifs to fill in the space and make the resulting fabric sturdier.

This can work with any motif. Just replace a chain for a slip stitch join as in the previous joining.

In the example shown, ch 7, sl st to 1st ch to make a ring. Ch 2, 2 dc-cl in ring, *ch 1, sl st to adjoining motif's picot, ch 1, 3 dc-cl in ring, ch 1, sl st to adjoining motif's next picot, ch 1, 3 dc-cl in ring; rep from * for each additional motif around, sl st to 2 dc-cl, fasten off.

This fancy seam adds a little vine of clusters between motifs. It looks quite impressive on both solid and lacy motifs.

Align the edges of the motifs side-by-side, and join the yarn to the edge of the motif on the right.

Ch 1, sc in same sp as join, *ch 3, 2 dc-cl in 1st ch, sc in next ch-sp (or sk 3 sts and sc in next st) on motif on left, ch 3, 2 dc-cl in 1st ch, sc in next ch-sp (or sk 3 sts and sc in next st) on motif on right; rep from * across, fasten off.

This technique to join two motifs is very commonly used in Belgium lace crochet. Here it adds a bit of lace to solid motifs to transform them into a completely different fabric.

V-Stitch (v-st): [2 dc, ch 1, 2 dc] in same st or sp.

Align the edges of the motifs side-by-side, and join the yarn to the motif on the left; chain 7.

Row 1 (RS): Sl st to corner of motif on right, sk 3 ch, v-st in next ch, ch 1, sl st to motif on left (about the same height as row 1), turn.
Row 2: Ch 1, v-st in ch-1 sp on v-st, ch 1, sk 3 sts on right motif, sl st to next st of motif on right, turn.
Row 3: Ch 1, v-st in ch-1 sp on v-st, ch 1, sl st to motif on the left, turn.
Rep rows 2 and 3 up the motifs to the end, fasten off.

This is the simplest of all edgings, but is quite effective for finishing off a project.

Reverse Single Crochet (rev sc): Insert hk back into prev st (to the right in the clockwise direction), yo, pull up lp twisting hk around to face upward, yo, pull through sts on hk.

This stitch pattern is a multiple of any number of stitches. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, rev sc in ea sc around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description:
When you change the colors and slightly shift the last round, the edging almost looks corded or braided.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 3 plus 1 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 3 plus 1, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc in 1st sc, *ch 5, sk 2 sc, sc in next sc; rep from * to corner, ch 5, turn project 90 degrees, sc in next sc, cont around sl st to 1st sc, fasten off color, turn.
Rnd 3: Join new color to rnd 1 with sl st next to a skipped sc on row 2. Rep rnd 2 directions around, fasten off.

stitches.description:
This edging comes in handy when you need something simple and delicate.

Picot: Ch 3, sl st in 1st ch.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 3 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 3, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, *[sc in next 2 sc, picot, sc in next sc] across to corner, [sc, picot, sc, picot, sc] in corner sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description:
This wide edging adds a whole new dramatic finish to blankets or towels.

Single Crochet Spike (sc spike): Insert hk into st indicated 2 rows below, yo, pull up a lp, insert hk into st indicated 4 rows below, yo, pull up a lp, insert hk into st indicated 2 rows below, yo, pull up a lp, yo, pull through all lps on hk.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 6 plus 3 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (KS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 6 plus 3, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, *sc in ea sc to corner, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnds 3 and 4: Rep rnd 2 twice more, fasten off, turn.
Rnd 5: Join contrasting color, ch 1, sc in 1st sc, *[sc spike over (prev sc 2 sts away, next sc, sc 2 sts away), sc in next 5 sc] across to corner, [sc, sc spike over (prev sc 2 sts away, corner sc, sc 2 sts away), sc] in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc; fasten off.

stitches.description:
This simple edging is easy to memorize, making for a super-quick option.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 4 plus 3 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 2 (this counts as a half double crochet).
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Hdc across edge in a multiple of 4 plus 3, 3 hdc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc in top of t-ch, *ch 4, sk 3 hdc, sc in next hdc) across to corner hdc, ch 4, sk corner hdc, sc in next hdc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc in 1st sc, *ch 6, sk ch-4 sp, sc in next sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc in 1st sc, *ch 8, sk ch-6 sp, sc in next sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, sc in 1st sc, *ch 9, sk ch-8 sp, sc in next sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description:
This adorable edging adds a bit of whimsy to any project.

Picot: Ch 3, sl st in 1st ch.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 3 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 3, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 2 (counts as hdc), hdc in next 2 sc, *[picot, hdc in next 3 sc] across to 1 sc before corner sc, picot, hdc in sc before corner, [2 hdc, picot, 2 hdc] in corner sc, hdc in next sc; rep from * around, sl st to top of t-ch, do not turn.
Rnd 3: Sl st to next 2 hdc, ch 1, sc in same hdc, *ch 5, sk 2 hdc sc, sc in next hdc; rep from * to corner, ch 7 sk 2 corner hdc; sc in next hdc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description:
Even though this edging has a number of rounds, they are easily repeatable. When you stack these repeatable rounds, the product is quite stunning as an edging or entire pattern.

Picot: Ch 3, sl st in 1st ch.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 6 plus 3 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 6 plus 3, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 2: Sl st in next sc, ch 2, 2 hdc-cl in same sc, *[ch 2, sk 2 sc, 3 hdc-cl in next sc] across to corner, ch 2, [3 hdc-cl, ch 3, 3 hdc-cl] in corner sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st 2 hdc-cl, turn.
Rnd 3: Sl st in next ch-2 sp, ch 2, 2 hdc-cl in same ch-2 sp, *[ch 2, 3 hdc-cl in next ch-2 sp] across to corner, ch 2, [3 hdc-cl, ch 3, 3 hdc-cl] in corner ch-3 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st 2 hdc-cl, turn.
Rnds 4 and 5: Rep rnd 3 twice.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, *3 sc in ea ch-2 sp to corner, 5 sc in ch-3 sp at corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 7: Ch 1, *sc in ea sc to corner sc, 3 sc in corner sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 8: Sl st in next 4 sc, sc in next sc, *[ch 3, picot, ch 3, sk 5 sc, sc in next sc] across to sc 2 sts from corner sc, ch 3, picot, ch 3, sk 3 sc at corner, sc in next sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 9: Ch 1, sc in 1st sc, *ch 4, picot, ch 4, sc in next sc; rep from * across to corner ch-sp, ch 5, picot, ch 5, sc in next sc; rep from * around, fasten off.

stitches.description:
This lacy edging is a simple chain lace with just a bit of fancy clusters on the final round.

Picot: Ch 3, sl st in 1st ch.

Cluster: *Yo, insert hk into st indicated, yo, pull up lp, yo, pull through 2 lps on hk, yo, insert hk into same st, yo, pull up lp, yo, pull through 2 lps on hk; rep from * in next st indicated, yo, pull through all lps on hk.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 3 plus 2 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 3 plus 2, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc in next sc, *ch 5, sk corner sc, sc in next sc, [ch 4, sk 2 sc, sc in next sc] across to sc before corner; rep from * around to 1st corner ch-sp, ch 2, hdc in 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc in hdc, *[ch 4, sc in next ch-4 sp] across corner ch-sp, ch 4, [sc, ch 7, sc] in ch-5 sp; rep from * around to 1st sc, ch 2, hdc in 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, sc in hdc, ch 4, *[sc, ch 9, sc] in corner ch-sp, [ch 4, sc in next ch-4 sp] across to corner ch-sp; rep from * around to 1st sc, ch 2, hdc in 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 5: Ch 2, cluster in 1st and next ch-sp, *ch 2, picot, ch 2, cluster in prev and next ch-sp; rep from * to corner ch-sp, ch 2, picot, ch 2, cluster in prev and corner ch-sp, ch 2, picot, ch 2, [4 dc-cl, ch 2, picot, ch 2, 4 dc-cl] in corner ch-sp, ch 2, picot, ch 2, cluster in corner ch-sp and next ch-4 sp; rep from * around, ch 2, picot, ch 2, sl st to 1st cluster, turn.

stitches.description:
The shells on the final round of this edging are highlighted not only in color but also by being separated by the simple rounds.

Shell (sh): [(Dc, ch 1) 3 times, dc] in st indicated.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 8 plus 5 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 8 plus 5 to corner, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next dc, *5 dc in corner sc, dc in ea sc to corner; rep from * around, sl st to top of t-ch, turn.
Rnd 3: Sl st in next dc, ch 2, sk 1 sc, *[ch 2, sk 2 dc, sc in next 2 dc] across to corner sc 2 away from corner, ch 2, 2 sc in next dc, 3 sc in corner sc, 2 sc in next dc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 4: Ch 1, *[2 sc in next ch-2 sp, sc in next 2 sc] across to corner, 2 sc in next ch-2 sp, 3 sc in next sc, sc in next sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, sc in next 2 sc, *[ch 2, sk 2 sc, sc in next 2 sc] across to corner sc 3 away from corner, ch 2, sk 2 sc, sc in next sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 6: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in ch-2 sp, *[dc in next 2 dc in ch-2 sp] across to corner dc, dc in next 2 sp, 5 dc in corner dc at top of t-ch, fasten off.
Rnd 7: With RS facing, join contrasting color to dc 3 after a corner st, *sc in ea dc across to corner, 3 sc in corner dc; rep from * around, do not turn.
Rnd 8: Ch 1, [sc, ch 3, sc] in same sc, *sk 3 sc, [sc, ch 3, sc] in next sc, sk 3 sc, [sc, ch 3, sc] in next sc, sk 3 sc, [dc, ch 3, sc] in next sc; rep from * around to corner, [dc, ch 3, sc] in last [dc, ch 3, sc] in next sc, sk 3 sc, [sc, ch 3, sc] in next sc, sk 3 sc, [sc, ch 3, sc] in next sc; rep from * around, omitting last [sc, ch 3, sc], sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description:
You can completely block this fancy edging to its last round for a classic look. If you leave its last round unblocked, it will ruffle slightly.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 8 plus 1 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 8 plus 1 st, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next sc, *[dc, ch 7, dc] in corner sc, dc in next 4 sc, [ch 5, sk 3 sc, dc in next 5 sc] rep to corner; rep from * around, sl st to top of t-ch, turn.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc in top of t-ch, *[4 dc, ch 2, 4 dc] in next ch-5 sp, sk 2 dc, sc in next dc, sk 2 dc; rep from * to corner, [(4 dc, ch 2) twice, 4 dc] in corner ch-sp, sk 2 dc, sc in next dc, sk 2 dc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 4: Sl st in next dc, turn, ch 2, dc in next dc (sk sc), ch 1, *sk next dc, dc in next dc, ch 1, sk next dc, [dc, ch 2, dc] in next ch-2 sp, ch 1, sk next dc, dc in next dc, ch 1, dc btw next 2 dc, ch 1, dc in next dc, ch 1, [dc, ch 2, dc] in next ch-2 sp, ch 1, sk next dc, dc in next dc, ch 1, sk next dc, dc2tog in next 2 dc (sk sc), ch 1, **sk next dc, dc in next dc, ch 1, sk next dc, [dc, ch 2, dc] in next ch-2 sp, ch 1, sk next dc, dc in next dc, ch 1, sk next dc, dc2tog in next 2 dc (sk sc), ch 1; rep from ** to corner; rep from * around to beg, sl st in 1st dc, turn.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, *[sc in next ch-1 sp, ch 2] twice, [sc, ch 4, sc] in next ch-2 sp, [ch 2, sc in next ch-1 sp] twice, ch 2; rep from * to corner, [sc in next ch-1 sp, ch 2] twice, [sc, ch 4, sc] in next ch-2 sp, [ch 2, sc in next ch-1 sp] twice, ch 4; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description:
This sweet little edging adds a touch of cluster charm to any project.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 6 on the sides. Join the yarn 3 sts before the corner to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 6, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Sl st to next sc, ch 1, sc in same sc, *ch 2, sk 2 sc, [sc, ch 5, sc] in corner sc, **ch 2, sk 2 sc, sc in next sc, ch 3, sk 2 sc, sc in next ** sc; rep from ** to 2 sts from corner sc; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 3: Sl st in next ch-3 sp, ch 3, [2 dc-cl, ch 3, 3 dc-cl] in same ch-3 sp, *[3 dc-cl, ch 3, 3 dc-cl] in ea ch-3 sp across, [3 dc-cl, ch 3, 3 dc-cl] in corner ch-5 sp, [(3 dc-cl) 3 times 3 dc-cl] in corner ch-5 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 2nd ch of t-ch, turn.
Rnd 4: Sl st to next 3 dc-cl, sl st to next ch-3 sp, [(sc, ch 2) 4 times, sc] in ea ch-3 sp around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description:
This exotic edging mixes solid shells with large chain spaces twice for a stacked and dramatic effect.

Picot: Ch 3, sl st in 1st ch.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 9 plus 3 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 9 plus 3, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 7 (counts as dc, ch-4 sp), sk 2 sc, *[dc, ch 6, dc] in corner sc, **ch 4, sk 2 sc, dc in next sc; rep from ** to 2 st from corner sc, ch 4, sk 2 sc; rep from * around to t-ch, ch 2, hdc in 3rd ch of t-ch (counts as ch-4 sp), turn.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc around post of hdc, *[3 dc, ch 2, 3 dc] in next ch-4 sp, sc in next ch-4 sp, ch 4, sc in next ch-4 sp; rep from * to corner ch-5 sp, [3 dc, ch 2, 6 dc, ch 2, 3 dc] in ch-5 sp, sc in next ch-4 sp, ch 4, sc in next ch-4 sp; rep from * around to 1st sc, ch 2, hdc in 1st sc (counts as ch-4 sp), turn.
Rnd 4: Ch 7 (counts as dc, ch-4 sp), *dc in ch-2 sp, ch 4, [dc, ch 4, dc] in btw 3rd and 4th dc, ch 4, dc in ch-2 sp, **ch 4, [dc, ch 4, dc] in next ch-4 sp, ch 4, dc in next ch-2 sp; rep from ** to corner; rep from * around to last ch-2 sp, ch 4, dc in last ch-4 sp, ch 4, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, turn.
Rnd 5: Sl st in ch-4 sp, ch 3 (counts as dc), [2 dc, ch 2, picot, ch 2, 3 dc] in same ch-4 sp, ch 2, *sc in next dc, picot, ch 2, sk next ch-4 sp, [3 dc, ch 2, picot, ch 2, 3 dc] in next ch-4 sp, ch 2, sk next ch-4 sp; rep from * across to corner ch-4 sps, sc in next dc, picot, ch 4, sk next ch-4 sp, [3 dc, ch 2, picot, ch 2, 3 dc] in next ch-4 sp, ch 4, sk next ch-4 sp; rep from * around, sc in next dc, picot, ch 2, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten off.

stitches.description:
The picots the shells on this edging are reminiscent of time gone by.

Picot: Ch 3, sl at to 1st ch.

Shell (sh): [(2 dc, picot) twice, dc, (picot, 2 dc) twice] in st indicated.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 7 plus 5 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 7 plus 5, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 2: Ch 1, sc in 1st sc, *3 sc in corner sc, sc in ea sc across to corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 3: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc in next 4 sc, *ch 4, sk 2 sc, dc in next 5 sc; rep from * across to corner, ch 4, sk 2 sc, [dc, ch 4, dc] in corner sc; rep from * around, ch 2, hdc in top of t-ch, turn.
Rnd 4: Ch 4 (counts as tr), 2 tr in 1st ch-sp, *ch 5, sk corner ch-4 sp, [3 tr, ch 5, 3 tr] in next ch-4 sp and ea ch-sp across to corner ch-sp; rep from * around to 1st ch-sp, 3 tr in 1st ch-sp, ch 5, sl st to top of t-ch, turn.
Rnd 5: Sl st in 1st ch-5 sp, ch 3 (counts as dc), [dc, picot, 2 dc, picot, dc, (picot, 2 dc) twice] in same ch-5 sp, sh in ea ch-5 sp around, sl st to top of t-ch, fasten off.

stitches.description:
This delicate edging looks much more complex and adds a dramatic ending to any project.

Picot: Ch 3, sl st in 1st ch.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 3 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 3, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 2: Sl st in next sc, ch 1, *[sc, ch 5, sc] in corner sc, **ch 5, sk 3 sc, sc in next sc; rep from ** across to corner, ch 5, sk 3 sc; rep from * around ch 2, dc in 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc in dc, *[ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp] twice, 7 dc in next ch-5 sp, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * across to last 3 ch-5 sps before corner, [sc in next ch-5 sp, ch 5] twice, sc in next ch-5 sp, 9 dc in corner ch-5 sp, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, turn.
Rnd 4: Sl st to 1st dc, ch 4 (counts as dc, ch-1 sp), *[dc, ch 1] in ea dc to last dc, dc in last dc, sc in next ch-5 sp, ch 5, sc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 3rd ch of t-ch, turn.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, *[(sc, ch 2) 4 times, sc] in next ch-5 sp, [2 sc, ch 2] in ea ch-1 sp to last, 2 sc in last ch-1 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.

stitches.description:
The layered edging adds a sweet-as-can-be touch to afghans or skirts.

The stitch pattern is a multiple of 4 plus 2 on the sides. Join the yarn to the edge with a sl st, and ch 1.
stitches.instructions:
Large Outside Edging
Rnd 1 (RS): *Sc across edge in a multiple of 4 plus 2, 3 sc in corner; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 2: Sl st in next 2 sc, ch 3 (counts as dc), dc-blp in next sc, *ch 3, sk 2 sc, dc-blp in next 2 sc; rep from * across to 3rd st from corner sc, ch 3, sk 2 sc, dc-blp in next sc, [dc-blp, ch 3, dc-blp] in corner sc, dc-blp in next sc; rep from * around, ch 1, hdc to top of t-ch, do not turn.
Rnd 3: Ch 1, sc around hdc, *ch 5, sc in next ch-3 sp; rep from * across to corner ch-sp, ch 5, [sc, ch 5, sc] in corner ch-sp; rep from * around to 1st sc, ch 2, dc in 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 4: Ch 3 (counts as dc), dc around dc post, *ch 3, 2 dc in next ch-5 sp; rep from * across to corner ch-sp, ch 3, [2 dc, ch 3, 2 dc] in corner ch-sp; rep from * around to t-ch, ch 1, hdc in top of t-ch, do not turn.
Rnd 5: Ch 1, sc around hdc post, *ch 5, sc in next ch-3 sp; rep from * across to corner ch-sp, ch 5, [sc, ch 5, sc] in corner ch-sp; rep from * around, ch 5, sl st to 1st sc, do not turn.
Rnd 6: Ch 1, *4 sc in next ch-5 sp, [(ch 3, dc, ch 3, sl st) 3 times] in 4th sc, 3 sc in same ch-5 sp; rep from * around, sl st to 1st sc, fasten off.
Small Inside Edging
